name: Jamf Pro REST API
what_it_does: Enterprise Apple MDM platform with comprehensive REST and Classic APIs for managing thousands of Apple devices — macOS, iOS, iPadOS, tvOS. Market-leading solution for enterprise Apple deployments.
use_cases: Automating macOS device enrollment and software deployment at enterprise scale
Querying device inventory, hardware specs, and compliance status programmatically
Managing smart groups, policies, and configuration profiles via API
Triggering MDM commands (lock, wipe, restart, update) on managed devices
Integrating with ITSM/CMDB systems for Apple device lifecycle management
not_for: Small businesses — pricing and complexity favors enterprise
Organizations wanting a simple MDM without extensive customization
best_when: An agent manages large enterprise Apple fleets (1000+ devices) requiring advanced policy management, compliance reporting, and ITSM integration.
avoid_when: You have fewer than 100 Apple devices — Kandji or Mosyle are simpler and cheaper.

auth.notes: Dual API: Classic API uses Basic auth; Pro API uses OAuth2/Bearer tokens. API roles define granular permissions. Client credentials flow for service accounts. Token-based auth recommended for automation.

pricing.paid_tiers: From ~$10-15/device/year for Jamf Now; Jamf Pro enterprise pricing on request (typically $100k+/year)
pricing.notes: Jamf Pro is enterprise pricing. Jamf Now is for SMB. Trial available. Pricing not publicly listed for Jamf Pro — requires sales contact. Well-established market leader.

performance.rate_limits: Throttling varies by endpoint; generally generous for enterprise accounts

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Developer portal with API reference for both Classic and Pro APIs. Classic API is XML-based and older. Pro API is JSON REST. Error messages vary by endpoint — Pro API is more consistent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Resource creation can produce duplicates — use external IDs or lookup before create. MDM commands are queued and idempotent within management state.
known_agent_gotchas: Two parallel APIs (Classic XML and Pro REST) — agents must know which to use for each operation
Classic API returns XML; Pro API returns JSON — response parsing differs significantly
MDM commands are async — device must check in to execute commands
Bearer tokens expire after 30 minutes — agents need token refresh logic

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP Moderate authorized for Jamf Cloud. SOC2 Type II. ISO 27001. EU data residency available. Apple MDM protocol with certificate-based device identity. Role-based API access control.
